steps.title: Express the given condition in exponential form
steps.content:
Since $f(x) = c^x$, we have $f(8) = c^8$ and $f(6) = c^6$. The condition $f(8) = 16 \cdot f(6)$ becomes:
$$c^8 = 16 \cdot c^6$$

steps.title: Divide by $c^6$ and solve for $c$
steps.content:
Dividing both sides by $c^6$ (valid since $c \gt 1$ means $c^6 \gt 0$):
$$c^2 = 16$$
Taking the square root and using $c \gt 1$:
$$c = 4$$
